name: apscheduler
description: Advanced Python Scheduler - Task scheduling and job queue system
capabilities: Schedule Python functions with cron, interval, and date triggers
Run jobs synchronously or asynchronously with FastAPI integration
Persist schedules across application restarts with SQL databases
Distribute work across scheduler and worker nodes
Manage job results and handle job events
Configure thread pool and process pool executors
author: bossjones
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing code examples for the APScheduler Python library. All 82 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The analyzer misinterpreted markdown code fences (backticks) as shell command execution, placeholder URLs as hardcoded endpoints, standard Python keywords as cryptographic algorithms, and job scheduling patterns as system reconnaissance. No actual executable code, network calls, or file operations exist in this skill. It is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Automate recurring tasks and background jobs in Python applications. APScheduler provides cron-style, interval-based, and date-based scheduling with support for async execution and multiple persistence backends.
limitations: Requires Python 3.9 or higher for async features
Memory-based scheduler loses jobs on application restart
Does not provide built-in task deduplication
Monitoring and metrics require custom implementation

useCases.title: Automated report generation
useCases.description: Schedule daily, weekly, or monthly report generation jobs that run without manual intervention

useCases.title: Periodic data sync
useCases.description: Sync data from external APIs or databases at regular intervals with configurable triggers

useCases.title: Health check monitoring
useCases.description: Run background monitoring tasks that check system health and trigger alerts

promptTemplates.title: Basic scheduling
promptTemplates.prompt: Use APScheduler to run my cleanup function every minute with an interval trigger

promptTemplates.title: Cron scheduling
promptTemplates.prompt: Schedule a function to run at 9 AM every weekday using cron trigger

promptTemplates.title: With persistence
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up AsyncScheduler with PostgreSQL persistence so jobs survive application restarts

promptTemplates.title: Distributed workers
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a distributed APScheduler setup with separate scheduler and worker nodes using AsyncpgEventBroker

bestPractices: Use persistent data stores in production to survive application restarts
Set appropriate misfire_grace_time to handle system delays gracefully
Subscribe to job events for monitoring and alerting on job failures
antiPatterns: Storing credentials directly in job kwargs - use environment variables instead
Scheduling very short intervals without proper coalesce configuration
Ignoring job exceptions without error handling or retry policies

faq.question: How does APScheduler compare to Celery?
faq.answer: APScheduler is lighter and simpler for single-application scheduling. Celery is better for complex distributed task queues.

faq.question: Can I run APScheduler in FastAPI?
faq.answer: Yes, use the lifespan pattern with AsyncScheduler to run the scheduler alongside your FastAPI application.

faq.question: What happens to jobs when the app restarts?
faq.answer: With in-memory storage, jobs are lost. Use SQLAlchemyDataStore or MongoDBDataStore to persist jobs across restarts.

faq.question: How do I run jobs in the background?
faq.answer: Call scheduler.start_in_background() after adding your schedules. This runs the scheduler loop without blocking.

faq.question: Can I pass arguments to scheduled functions?
faq.answer: Yes, use args for positional arguments and kwargs for named arguments in add_schedule or add_job.

faq.question: What executors are available?
faq.answer: ThreadPoolExecutor (default, good for I/O tasks) and ProcessPoolExecutor (good for CPU-intensive tasks).
